title: Stay Fit 4 - | Ps. Andrés Spyker ft. Phil Dooley
transcript:
Hola a todos, esto es Stay Fit, estamos haciendo esto en inglés hoy.
Mi nombre es Andrés y esto es Pastor Phil Dooley
de South Africa.
Hola, voy a hacer esto en español.
Hola, ¿cómo estás?
Bien.
Y eso es sobre donde está.
Es un podcast muy corto, si lo hicimos en español conmigo.
Exacto, exactamente.
Te dirías, así, tacos, amigos, nachos.
Es a lo largo de tu boca.
Muchísimas gracias.
So, Pastor Phil es con nosotros en Efecto 2018 en Morelia, México.
¿Estás disfrutando la ciudad, la conferencia?
Bueno, la segunda vez que he estado aquí y lo amo.
Creo que la primera vez que he estado aquí,
conociendo a ustedes, conociendo un poco de la conferencia y la ciudad.
Y a esta hora me siento mucho más confiante,
creo, porque habiendo estado aquí antes y,
sí, ya sabes, estamos realmente disfrutando.
I mean, Morelia.
We enjoyed tacos last night.
We had tacos.
We went to hell.
That's a taco place, it's called hell.
Yeah, we need to qualify that, but we went to hell.
We kept going.
We didn't stay in hell.
You went through hell.
We went through hell.
That's right.
And we had our tacos and I ate way too many,
but that's all right, because this happens sometimes after you preach.
That's awesome.
And yeah, we're enjoying it.
Listen, your preachings have been amazing in Efecto.
Thank you.
People have connected real well with you and your message.
Thank you.
About loving our city, building our city and changing our city through just social
work, one-on-one.
Everything you've shared has just been so amazing.
So thank you.
Thank you.
It's a message that I know is helped in South Africa a lot.
Yes.
And I know we need it in Mexico.
It's been really great.
Well, I feel like as we've been talking, you know, there is definitely some similarities
between South Africa and Mexico and Latin America, because you do have a disparity of wealth
and poverty.
And the church, I believe, has a significant role to play in the middle of that.
We want to reach everybody, but that the church can be a catalyst for bringing about change in
some of the social challenges that we're facing in society today.
That we shouldn't just sit by watching or, you know, looking out on and going, that's
bad and the government isn't doing this or, you know, business isn't doing that.
But we actually have to take responsibility.
And so, yeah, I believe that what God is stirring in our hearts in South Africa
has relevance to what is happening here in Latin America.
That's so good.
We've felt the connection.
We've felt the deep impact, you know, in our hearts, what you're sharing.
So it's been great.
I just want to thank you for...
I know it's hard to come all the way to Mexico.
It's not close.
It's not close at all.
But it's worth it.
I want to thank you.
And it's definitely worth it with regard to some of the feedback that I've received
and some of the different people even talking to a friend that you introduced me to.
Yeah, Paco.
Yeah, who has a young man with a dream to, you know, create prosthetic limbs for those
who couldn't necessarily afford that.
And, you know, telling me about...
Kids with disabilities who are poor.
Yeah, that's right.
And using 3D printing and different technology.
I'm like, wow, there are some people with a vision and a dream.
And using their creativity.
So good.
Under, I believe, you know, the Holy Spirit to make significant change, real change.
So that gets me excited.
So I believe there's going to be lots of fruit from your visit here.
And so we're blessed.
And anyway, there is a blog I have on a video blog on Stay Fit.
And it sounds good.
And it's just, you know, I just started...
A few years ago I started to do exercise on and off.
Yes.
And then, starting the year, I just got real serious about...
Yes.
Actually, you're the one, last December we talked.
And I told you, yeah, I'm running every now and then.
Yeah.
And you said, you know, you've hit 40.
Yes.
And you need to start lifting weights.
Come on.
Because you can lose muscle mass.
Yes.
So that sort of bugged me a little bit.
So that was my worst challenge.
Yes, sir.
When we were in Guatemala.
In Guatemala, last December.
So I came back in January and I started to hit the gym hard.
Good.
And so, you know, I've lost several kilos.
Come on.
Some muscle mass.
You're looking strong.
My goodness.
Looking a little better.
Come on.
And so, you know, I'm going...
And so I've developed this idea, a hashtag,
and then it became an idea of stay fit.
Yes.
Not get fit.
Yes.
But stay fit.
I love it.
Because many times I get fit and then not fit.
And so I've developed it into a lifestyle of eating well,
having good family, staying fit with my family,
staying fit with God, staying fit in my emotions,
staying fit in my body.
Yes.
Can you ask you how do you stay fit in general, you know?
Well, look, I think many times when we're young,
we take these things for granted.
Right.
You know, we take our health for granted.
We just kind of assume everything's going to be fine
for all of our days.
But as we get older, I think we begin to realize
that if we don't look after ourselves,
some of those things that we desire to do and achieve
become more difficult.
Yeah, wow.
If we don't deal with emotional baggage,
then we become jaded and we can become bitter or,
you know, and just the way we relate to people
isn't healthy.
Yeah.
And in our physical life, if we don't consider
where our body is and what's going on
and we don't look after our body,
again it can become more difficult to handle
the pace of life and be able to be proactive
rather than reactive.
You know, I'm always tired and I'm always this.
You know, God wants us proactive,
able to deal with challenges
and able to make positive choices.
And also in our spiritual life,
if we don't make positive choices there,
then we can become familiar
and then we can lose our edge spiritually.
So I think the word that is a word we don't like,
the English word is discipline.
Wow.
That we have to make choices to discipline ourselves.
We all hate that word, honestly.
Yes.
Why do we hate it so much?
Well, I think because it has connotations
that are often negative,
that it feels like discipline is about
doing everything we don't want to do.
Oh, wow.
But I've actually learned the opposite
and you're telling me the same.
Yeah.
That when you start something,
so if you start lifting weights,
the discipline of doing that regularly
just is hard.
Totally.
It's hard on a number of levels.
It's hard just lifting the weight.
It's hard because you maybe don't know
what to do properly.
You can feel like, you know...
It's a whole different world.
It's like you don't even know how to use a machine
or lift the weight properly.
It's annoying.
Yes.
But what happens is,
if you continue the discipline of that,
you actually gain momentum.
And when you gain the momentum
and you see the results,
it becomes more of a lifestyle.
And your body actually benefits from it.
Your body actually craves it
because they say that when you work out,
you release endorphins.
Totally.
And you feel at the end of it,
of a workout, you feel like this was painful.
But I feel somehow I feel good.
I feel a lot better.
Isn't it crazy?
It's awesome.
And so discipline, I think,
if we understand it correctly,
it's going to build positive things into your life.
So good.
There's a statement.
How do you get over the concept of,
how do you embrace discipline?
Do you think about the benefits?
Well, how do you overcome
that negative connotation on this thing?
I think you just have to,
there's no easy way.
I think, yes, you can look at
what it's going to do for you.
You just have to push through it.
Well, I think you just got to decide,
this is what I'm going to do.
So for example, if I go to the gym,
I try and have make myself
have less decisions than I have to.
Because I live in a world
where I have to make a lot of decisions.
Wow.
So for me, I know there's a class
at the gym I go to at 6 a.m.
and all I have to do is get myself up
at 5.30, help me Jesus.
Get to the gym at 6
and the class is set out.
Wow.
I don't have to go to the gym
and work out what am I going to do?
Right.
Am I going to do this?
Oh, I'm...
It's laid out for you.
Yeah, it's laid out.
So all I have to do is get up
and get there.
And I think when people make choices
that all I got to do is do this
and then the hardest part
about choosing to run
is probably putting on your shoes.
Totally.
Once you start running,
it's like, oh, that's not so bad.
Once you get into the workout,
it's not so bad.
The hardest part
is that initial ghetto.
So I'll ask you one last question.
I'll let you go.
I know that there's things to do.
But how do you...
Like we all hit low.
Yes.
You know, like when we're like...
It's just it's really hard
to get back into the rhythm
and you've lost your rhythm a bit.
You've had a holiday,
a break, a vacation.
Yeah, and it happens...
I think we have those low seasons
in all of our lives.
Yeah.
How do you bring yourself back
to rhythm?
Well, I think there's a couple of ways to do it.
I think one of the ways
is who you associate with.
Wow.
So I need to associate
with people who are going to inspire me.
Wow.
Because if I'm just hanging around people
who are not in any way challenging me.
Do you have a buddy that's like,
hey, let's go to the gym.
Where are you?
Why don't you wake up?
That kind of thing.
I have some people in my world
who will challenge me on those areas.
Wow.
But in a positive way.
Cool.
And I'll also do the same for them.
So we'll talk about,
oh, I did this workout
and they'll be like,
I did that workout.
And so what happens is,
you know,
you've got some people
who are actually motivating you.
That's cool.
So I think that's really helpful.
And I think generally
when we're in a lull
we can often end up just going to
people who aren't going to challenge us.
Yep.
And that's never going to help you
build your life.
So in all areas,
it's difficult or sometimes
we can feel insecure
when we're hanging around people
who have achieved more than us
in certain areas.
Totally.
But actually this is where we should be.
This is where we grow.
Craig Rochelle says it.
You should hang out with people
that make your brain hurt.
Yes.
Because you can't even comprehend
how they do what they do.
I completely agree.
Make your brain hurt.
Make your body hurt.
Because they train hard, you know.
And so whatever it is,
you need to find some people like that
and you need to just get around them.
That's awesome.
I think that's key.
I think just committing,
you know,
because you build momentum.
It's like the term inertia.
If you've got a car
that you have to get going,
maybe the engines,
you know,
you've run out of fuel, whatever.
The hardest part
is pushing that car to get going.
Once it's going,
you've just got to steer it.
And I think in any of these areas of our life,
the hardest part is just getting going.
Once you get that momentum,
it feeds off itself.
So if you're in a lull,
I would just say,
just tell your body,
body tomorrow morning,
we're getting up.
That's right.
And what happens is
when you put disciplines in one area,
I find it flows into other areas of your life.
I completely agree.
I've seen that happen.
Yeah.
So you become,
you're like disciplined in going to the gym.
Then you start to go,
well actually,
I want to eat differently
because I'm working out.
Right.
And so you start.
What happens is
you start working out and you're like,
why don't I see more progress
in a faster way?
And then you're like,
oh, I'm not eating right.
Yeah, yeah, yeah.
And then you see somebody post,
oh, it's 80% diet,
20% working out.
Yeah, yeah, yeah.
And you're like,
oh crap,
I'm doing 80% working out,
not eating well.
And so it pushes you.
Yeah,
and it challenges you
to grow in that area of your life.
And I find that
there are always.
So you would say,
sorry,
you would say laziness is contagious,
but also discipline
is contagious in other areas.
Absolutely.
And discipline equals freedom.
Yeah.
You know,
we think it's the opposite.
We think I want to be free.
Therefore,
I don't want to be undisciplined.
Wow.
But I find undisciplined people
are often the most bound.
Wow.
They get bound up
in financial problems
because they're undisciplined
with their money.
They get health issues
because they're undisciplined
in how they look
after themselves physically.
So good.
They're,
you know,
undisciplined spiritually
and so it causes
all kinds of baggage.
So good.
But you get disciplined
and it creates greater freedom.
So good.
Desculpa a lo que dices,
freedoms and freedom
in your physical wellbeing,
spiritual love.
Yeah.
The stronger you are,
you know,
our pastor used to say,
your spirit cannot go
where your body cannot take it.
Exactly.
And so the stronger
your body is,
you're more free
to take whatever God's giving.
Yeah,
absolutely.
That's awesome.
Phil, thank you so much.
Appreciate it.
I love what you guys are doing.
Love being with you
at Mas Vida
and excited for
all God is taking you guys
into the future.
Thank you so much
Nos vemos la próxima vez y ¡hasta la próxima!
